fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Illinois Audiologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a audiologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Illinois - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: audiologist license reciprocity illinois, how to transfer audiologist license to illinois, audiologist compact illinois, illinois audiologist endorsement application

board.name: Illinois Board of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
board.parent_dept: Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation (IDFPR)
board.mailing_address:
Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation
320 W. Washington Street
Springfield, IL 62786
board.physical_address: 320 W. Washington St., 3rd Floor, Springfield, IL 62786

license_types.title: AuD, CCC-A
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a audiologist in Illinois.

steps.title: Create an IDFPR Online Services Account
steps.description: Visit the IDFPR Online Services Portal and register for a new account if you do not already have one. This account will be used to submit your application, track its status, and manage your license.

steps.title: Complete the Audiologist Application
steps.description: Log into your IDFPR Online Services Portal account and complete the 'Application for Licensure - Audiologist'. Ensure all required sections are filled out accurately, including personal information, education, and examination details.

steps.title: Submit Required Documentation
steps.description: Upload or arrange for the submission of all necessary supporting documents directly through the IDFPR Online Services Portal or via mail if specified. This typically includes official transcripts, proof of ASHA CCC-A, and any required affidavits.

steps.title: Pay the Application Fee
steps.description: Pay the non-refundable application fee through the IDFPR Online Services Portal using a credit or debit card. The fee for licensure by examination or endorsement is currently $150.

steps.title: Pass the Praxis Exam (if applicable)
steps.description: Ensure official scores for the Audiology Praxis Examination (5343) are sent directly to IDFPR from ETS, if required for your application type (e.g., licensure by examination).

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly check the status of your application through your IDFPR Online Services Portal account. Respond promptly to any requests for additional information or clarification from IDFPR staff to avoid delays.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the Praxis. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Illinois audiologist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Illinois have reciprocity for audiologists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers audiologist practice in Illinois. To work in Illinois, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my audiologist license to Illinois?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Illinois while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Audiologist in Illinois
hero.dek: To apply for an Audiologist license in Illinois, you must utilize the Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation (IDFPR) Online Services Portal to submit your application and supporting documentation.

official_links.portal_name: Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation (IDFPR) Online Services Portal
official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the IDFPR Online Services Portal. Click 'Register for an Account' if you are a new user. Follow the prompts to create your account, providing personal information and security questions. Once registered, log in to access the online application system and select the appropriate Audiologist application.
